---
title: "Multiple Monitors Help.."
date: 2007-05-13
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by ratshell on 2007-05-13
Okay I have two video cards. A Nvidia Geforce 4 mmx agp and a Nvidia TNT PCI. The PCI boots first. Then starts the X session with STARTX. I want both montiors to work so I can drag a window back and forth. Wheather through TWINVIEW OR XINEROMA doesn't matter. Here is my current xorg.conf. 

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Nov  9 17:55:20 PST 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
    Option         "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
    Option         "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier     "primary"
Driver         "nvidia"
BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 0
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier "secondary"
Driver "vesa"
BusID "PCI:0:8:0"
Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "crt1"
    Option         "DPMS"
    HorizSync       28-64
    VertRefresh     43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "crt2"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       28-64
        VertRefresh     43-60
EndSection


Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "first"
    Device         "primary"
    Monitor        "crt1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP"
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       4
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       8
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       15
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       16
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "second"
        Device          "secondary"
        Monitor         "crt2"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "Default Layout"
Screen "first"
Screen "second" RightOf "first"
Option "Xinerama" "On"
InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
InputDevice "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
Mode 0666
EndSection

Any help would be greatly appreciated. I just want dual monitors to drag windows from one screen to the other.

---

### Post by 13east on 2007-05-14
i don't know how much this going to help you but:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=98456&highlight=tv-out+nvidia+notebook]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=98456&highlight=tv-out+nvidia+notebook")
this is the guide i've been using to connect my desktop to my tv.  i've been able to use this guide for tv-out for dapper, edgy and feisty.  hope this helps.

---

### Post by ratshell on 2007-05-14
Thanks for the help. But unfortunately that didn't help me with my situation. Thank you though. Help still needed.

---

### Post by dodgePT on 2007-05-14
I'm trying something similar, but with dual head, not two display adapters. What's your problem with the current configuration? You're unable to login to X?

The returned BusID for both adapters when you type 'lspci' is the same as in your xorg.conf?

---

### Post by ratshell on 2007-05-14
Well the problem with my current x configuration is that. Only one monitor comes on. The second one just stays blank. And yes the bus id's for the cards are right in the xorg configuration. Since they are two different cards they have two different bus ids. So I don't know what is causing the problem.

---

### Post by dodgePT on 2007-05-14
What's the output in /var/log/Xorg.log.0? You should be able to see the error there.

---

### Post by m@ra on 2007-05-15
Hi,

I have two dualhead graphic controllers nvidia 7100GS and some other integrated to my MSI motherboard. Though I enabled from BIOS to use both at the same time Ubuntu is willing to use only Nvidia. Not sure about the cause. Now I can have same picture from DVI-out and VGA which was primary target cause wanted to watch movies from TV...

I can see from log that your system anyway detects fine both cards. I would really look into some application that would allow configuration as detection should be fine as far as I can see.

SourceForge.net: xorg-edit ([http://sourceforge.net/projects/xorg-edit/](http://sourceforge.net/projects/xorg-edit/)) was great help for me.

---

### Post by Soybean on 2007-05-15
First, I should warn you, messing with X configurations still feels like voodoo to me. It's always been a bit of a "guess and test" procedure to get any sort of unusual setup working. But I can give you a couple of guesses, at least, and you can test them if you want. ;)

From your logs, it looks like there's some sort of problem with the configuration of your "secondary" screen (PCI:0:8:0). I assume that's the screen that isn't working? I'd try making an xorg.conf file that only uses that screen/device/monitor, and see what happens then. Right now, X seems to be saying, "oh, that screen doesn't work, but I've got this whole other screen to work with, so who cares?" So I'd take away the other screen and hope for a clearer error message.

Oh, but before going to all that trouble, you might try switching the "nvidia" driver to the "nv" driver and seeing what happens. I seem to recall that the nvidia drivers don't get along very well with Xinerama, so it seems worth a shot.

---

### Post by ratshell on 2007-05-15
Thx for the suggestion I will try that. And if I switch the configuration to only that other monitor with the vesa driver it works fine. So I know that card and monitor are good.

---

### Post by Nythain on 2007-05-15
nvidia drives get along great with xinerama... i use it
but im curious why one card is using nvidia drivers and another vesa, is it to old???
also i would throw your xinerama bit into its own section and not server layout
```
Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "1"
EndSection
```
but im not 100% sure that is to important
then i would try changing your server layout to look a bit more like this
```

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "first" 0 0
    Screen      1  "second" RightOf "first"

```

---

### Post by ratshell on 2007-05-15
I tried it and it is still a no go. The second monitor is still not coming on. Any other ideas would be appreciated. And it is using the vesa driver because it is to old to be supported by the nvidia driver.

---

### Post by Soybean on 2007-05-16
Well, I can make a couple more guesses...

1) What happens if you comment out the "Screen 0" and "Screen 1" lines in the Device sections? My understanding is that those lines are for selecting between multiple monitors on a single video card. So, it might be trying to find a second output port on your PCI:0:8:0 device, and that might be where the trouble is.

2) Based on this:
> (WW) NVIDIA(0): Option "UseDisplayDevice" requested "DFP", but no unused DFPs
(WW) NVIDIA(0): are available.
(II) NVIDIA(0): Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP" converted to "".
(WW) NVIDIA(0): Unable to find any of the requested display device "" in the
(WW) NVIDIA(0): list of available display devices "CRT-0".
I'd try commenting out the "UseDisplayDevice DFP" line. Not sure what a DFP is, but it doesn't seem to think you've got one. Or your DFP is busy, or something.

---

### Post by Nythain on 2007-05-16
DFP is digital flat panel... otherwise known as anything connecting via DVI input

---

### Post by ratshell on 2007-05-17
Tried that still no go .....

---
